name: Hiking around the castles of Ampúdia and Torremojón
name_original: Circular por los castillos de Ampúdia y Torremojón
region_intro: Hiking around Ampudia offers routes through the flat, agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos, characterized by open landscapes and historical sites. The region features gentle rolling terrain with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for various hiking abilities. Trails often connect charming villages and offer views of the Castilian countryside. The area's geology primarily consists of sedimentary plains, providing a consistent and accessible hiking environment. Best hiking trails around Ampudia The most popular hiking route is Castillos de Villalba de los Alcores y Montealegre de Campos — circular por la Tierra de Campos (GR 26), a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historical castles and open plains of the Tierra de Campos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senderos del Clarete: Trigueros del Valle - Quintanilla de Trigueros Loop, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail winds through vineyards and agricultural land, offering insights into the local wine region. Local hikers also love the Hiking around the castles of Ampúdia and Torremojón, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through historical villages and open countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Ampudia is defined by its open plains, historical castles, and agricultural landscapes, with routes offering options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Ampudia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Ampudia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ampudia?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes documented on komoot for the Ampudia area, offering a variety of experiences through the Tierra de Campos region.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for hiking in Ampudia?
A: Hiking around Ampudia primarily features flat, agricultural plains with gentle rolling terrain and minimal elevation changes. The trails often connect charming villages and offer expansive views of the Castilian countryside, making for a consistent and accessible hiking environment.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Ampudia?
A: Yes, Ampudia offers several easy hiking options. Out of the 14 available routes, 4 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ampudia?
A: The hiking routes in Ampudia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the open landscapes, historical sites, and the peaceful nature of the trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Ampudia?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085751304/castillos-de-villalba-de-los-alcores-y-montealegre-de-campos-circular-por-la-tierra-de-campos-gr-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Villalba de los Alcores y Montealegre de Campos — circular por la Tierra de Campos (GR 26)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail exploring historical castles. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077683331/senda-de-los-majuelos-circular-desde-trigueros-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Majuelos — circular desde Trigueros del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate loop.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Ampudia?
A: While the region is generally flat, you can find scenic spots offering expansive views of the Tierra de Campos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fields of Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Viewpoint (Autilla del Pino)&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights that provide panoramic vistas of the agricultural landscape.

Q: Are there historical sites or castles to explore along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The Ampudia region is rich in history and features several castles and historical villages. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Ampudia&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3744020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Trigueros del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villalba de los Alcores&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062473302/hiking-around-the-castles-of-ampudia-and-torremojon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking around the castles of Ampúdia and Torremojón&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to visit these historical landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ampudia?
A: The best time for hiking in Ampudia is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and the landscape is either blooming or showcasing autumnal colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though the flat terrain makes winter hiking feasible on clear days.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Ampudia suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the generally flat and gentle terrain of Ampudia makes many trails suitable for families with children. The easy routes, in particular, are a good choice for a leisurely family outing. Always check the specific route details for distance and duration to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ampudia?
A: Most trails in rural areas like Ampudia are generally dog-friendly, especially those through open countryside and agricultural land. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to carry water for them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: How long do hikes around Ampudia typically take?
A: Hikes around Ampudia vary in length and duration. Moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085751304/castillos-de-villalba-de-los-alcores-y-montealegre-de-campos-circular-por-la-tierra-de-campos-gr-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Villalba de los Alcores y Montealegre de Campos&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours 36 minutes for 10.9 miles (17.6 km), while shorter routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077683331/senda-de-los-majuelos-circular-desde-trigueros-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Majuelos&lt;/a&gt; are closer to 2 hours for 4.8 miles (7.8 km).

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Ampudia?
A: In Ampudia and the surrounding villages, you can typically find public parking areas, especially near town centers or local attractions, which often serve as convenient starting points for hikes. Specific parking availability may vary by trailhead, so it's recommended to check the route details or local maps for designated spots.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink along the hiking routes?
A: Many hiking routes in Ampudia connect or pass through charming villages. These villages often have local bars, restaurants, or small shops where you can find refreshments or a meal. Planning your route to include a stop in a village like Trigueros del Valle or Montealegre de Campos can enhance your hiking experience.
tips: Spanish medieval castle, now in ruins, located in the small town of the same name. It is located on the top of an isolated hill very close to the town, one of the foothills of the Torozos mountains.

Catholic temple of worship located in the small Palencia town of Torremormojón. Until the restoration work in 1986, the building was considered a work of the 16th century with Renaissance plasterwork and other masks.
It is the most important and best preserved fortification in the province of Palencia. It was built between the 13th and 15th centuries, being declared a national monument on July 3, 1931.
